channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Justice Minister Defends Anti-Terror and Hate Symbols Bill, Emphasizing Protections for Legitimate Protests
item.description: OTTAWA — Justice Minister Sean Fraser appeared before the Senate committee on human rights to discuss Bill C-9, which aims to introduce new criminal penalties for impeding or intimidating access to bu...

item.title: Lamido Urges Northern Leaders to Address Insecurity Through Moral Reform and State Police
item.description: Former Jigawa State Governor, Sule Lamido, don yarn say the North need to put their matter for order if dem wan stop the wahala of insecurity. For interview with Premier Radio, Kano, Lamido talk say b...

item.title: 16-Year-Old Boy Arrested in Nakuru for the Murder of 12-Year-Old Mercy Nyambura
item.description: Police in Njoro, Nakuru County have arrested a 16-year-old boy suspected of killing 12-year-old Mercy Nyambura, a Grade 6 pupil. The young girl was found dead inside a maize plantation hours after her...

item.title: Blind mother independently operates maize mill to support her family
item.description: A video of an elderly blind mother working at a maize mill has gone viral in Kenya, inspiring admiration and sympathy from netizens. Despite her disability, she performs her work independently without...

item.title: US expresses readiness to supply energy to India during upcoming visit
item.description: Ahead of his visit to India from May 23 to May 26, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that the United States is prepared to sell as much energy as India wishes to procure. Addressing reporters i...

item.title: Tanzanian Women Golfers Excel at Kenya Amateur Championship
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzanian women golfers made strong impression at the 2026 Kenya Ladies Amateur Strokeplay Championship held at Limuru Country Club. Neema Olomi of Arusha Gymkhana Club led the charge,...

item.title: Tulfo says Aplasca caused confusion among senators over supposed Senate attack
item.description: In a recent statement, Erwin Tulfo attributed responsibility to Mao Aplasca for the misunderstanding among senators who believed the Senate was under attack. Tulfo emphasized that Aplasca's actions or...

item.title: Foreign nationals at Diakonia Centre taken to Home Affairs for official verification in Durban
item.description: About 200 foreign nationals, including refugees and asylum seekers, who had sought shelter at the Diakonia Centre in Durban this week due to alleged threats and violence in their homes and workplaces,...

item.title: Temi Otedola and Mr Eazi Announce Expecting First Child
item.description: Nigerian actress and fashion influencer Temi Otedola and Afrobeats singer Mr Eazi, whose real name na Oluwatosin Ajibade, don announce say dem dey expect their first pikin. The couple drop the news fo...

item.title: Learning Pottery: Discovering Joy in the Creative Process
item.description: Years ago, the author attended a pottery class with her son, sparking an interest in ceramics despite initial clumsiness. Life commitments paused her pursuit, but now with more time, she enrols in a s...

item.title: Astrid Kirchherr and Stuart Sutcliffe: A Pioneering Love Story in Early Beatles History
item.description: The story of Astrid Kirchherr and Stuart Sutcliffe, often referred to as the “fifth Beatle,” is both romantic and tragic. Their relationship began in 1960 when Astrid, introduced to The Beatles by her...

item.title: Senator Mark Kelly Shows His NASA Spacesuit With a Hidden Feature in His Office
item.description: Senator Mark Kelly, a former NASA astronaut with a 15-year career, has placed the spacesuit he wore on the space shuttle on display in his office. Kelly, an Arizona Democrat, traveled to the Internati...

item.title: Kenya Plans to Move National Exams Online to Cut High Costs
item.description: Kenya's education system is preparing for a major shift as the Ministry of Education collaborates with the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) to gradually introduce electronic assessments in s...

item.title: Tanzania pushes for better care and support for people with disabilities and their caregivers
item.description: Disability rights advocates together with United Nations officials are urging the Tanzanian government to strengthen inclusive systems across social services, economic opportunities, and policy framew...

item.title: Oceana reports higher profits amid challenges from fuel, freight, and fishmeal markets
item.description: Oceana Group, the owner of household brand Lucky Star, has reported a rise in headline earnings per share by 7.7% to 349.8c for the six months ended March, despite a 6% drop in revenue to R4.9 billion...

item.title: Eswatini accepts US deportees amid legal and public concerns
item.description: Eswatini, a small kingdom in southern Africa, was one of the first countries to agree to host deported migrants under a US program initiated during Donald Trump's presidency. According to sources, Pri...

item.title: Trump undecided on attending son Donald Jr’s wedding in the Bahamas
item.description: US President Donald Trump has indicated he has not yet decided whether he will attend his son Donald Trump Jr’s wedding in the Bahamas over the Memorial Day long weekend. Speaking in the Oval Office, ...

item.title: GVMC proposes Electric Rapid Transit to reduce traffic on BRTS road
item.description: In a bid to modernise urban transport and tackle worsening traffic congestion along BRTS Road, the Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation (GVMC) has proposed an Electric Rapid Transit (e-RT) syst...

item.title: Home Office age assessments place hundreds of migrant children in adult accommodation
item.description: A recent report by the Helen Bamber Foundation has revealed that hundreds of migrant children arriving in the UK have been wrongly assessed as adults by the Home Office, putting them at considerable r...

item.title: New York Residents Can Enter Lottery for $50 FIFA World Cup Tickets
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has announced a special initiative allowing city residents to purchase FIFA World Cup tickets for just $50. A total of 1,000 tickets have been secured through an agr...
